work.title: Tensile Strength of Ropes and Friction Hitch Used in Tree Climbing Work

entries.reason: Complete hashed official article source; all pages manually reviewed, with figures, table layouts, references, brands, standards detail, recipes, and overgeneralization excluded.
Canonical page and licence statement reviewed through read-only browser rendering; shell denial body recorded but never treated as article content.
Verified resolution to canonical MDPI article; DOI recall excluded from QA.
Used only to verify attribution, licence link, indication of changes, no-endorsement, and no-additional-restrictions metadata.
Used solely to validate compatible rights metadata; no legal-code text is direct training content.

word_count_method: Unicode regex [\w\u2019'-]+ over CORPUS.md; whitespace count recorded separately
category_scope_note: The hardpoint and suspension mappings contain only comparative bench-study interpretation and explicit non-transfer boundaries. They offer no tying method, rating, recommendation, field clearance, natural-fiber result, bondage instruction, or human-suspension approval.

evidence_scope.design: 36 slow-pull semi-static bench runs in a balanced two-rope by two-hitch design with nine runs per treatment
evidence_scope.specimens: Axis 11 mm polyester-polyamide and XTC 16 nominal 13 mm polyamide work ropes with 8 mm polyester-Technora hitch cord
evidence_scope.observation_window: initial state and five 50 mm increments over a 250 mm machine stroke
evidence_scope.measurements: applied load, hitch slip, rope-diameter change, start-end near-hitch temperature, ambient temperature, and humidity
evidence_scope.analysis: factorial multivariate comparisons, post-hoc comparisons, one-factor temperature analysis, and separate plus combined regressions
evidence_scope.retained_results: hitch effect on load and slip; rope effect on diameter; observed extrema; treatment trends; partial regression relationships; nonsignificant between-treatment endpoint-temperature comparison
evidence_scope.excluded: figures, copied tables, manufacturers, catalog ratings, instruments, hardware, standards identifiers, operational setup, full equations, third-party results, dynamic or cyclic inference, safe ratings, recommendations, and cross-domain transfer

qa_derivation_policy.allowed: factorial design, treatment identities, replication structure, bench window, measured outcomes, comparative factor effects, descriptive observations, partial regressions, temperature interpretation, preliminary purpose, and explicit transfer limitations
qa_derivation_policy.forbidden: author order, DOI digit, URL, publication date, journal-coordinate, manufacturer, brand, instrument, hardware, catalog rating, standard, figure, table, reference, file hash, citation order, tying, assembly, preload, connection, climbing, suspension, rescue, safe rating, breaking-strength generalization, best-knot ranking, equipment recommendation, natural-fiber transfer, bondage transfer, or human-suspension approval

sampling_guidance.source_weighting: one DOI source-family document, not one document per treatment, result, table, displacement point, or numerical value
sampling_guidance.recommended_unit: chunks retaining exact specimens, hitch treatment, 36-run structure, slow semi-static 250 mm bench boundary, and no-rating or no-transfer limitation
sampling_guidance.avoid: isolating a load or slip number from its observed-bench status; converting statistical significance to safety; ranking hitches; treating repeated observations as equipment diversity; or separating any result from the dynamic, cyclic, natural-fiber, bondage, and human-suspension exclusions

document_disjoint_requirement: Assign the official article HTML identity, official PDF, DOI record, this Markdown, and every derivative to one source-family split before chunking or derivation.
protected_split_requirement: Exclude QA, dataset-review, dataset, trainer, training-projection, evaluation, validation, holdout, OOD, shadow, benchmark, probe, unrelated-corpus, sealed-holdout, and protected-QA documents from every training layer.
